_comment: Scheduled tasks. Koda's scheduler reads this file and runs each task on its cron schedule. Copy to ~/.koda/tasks.json and customize for your workflow.
_format: Each task has: prompt (what Koda should do), cron (when), type (silent | approval), optional timeout (seconds), optional chain (next task name), optional limits (maxTurns, maxBudgetUsd).
_cron_reference: Standard cron: 'minute hour day month weekday'. Examples: '0 9 * * *' = 9am daily, '0 */6 * * *' = every 6 hours, '0 20 * * 1' = 8pm every Monday.

daily_observation_review.prompt: Read the last 24 hours of observations.md and summarize the 3 most important things that happened. If anything needs follow-up, use propose_task() to queue it.

weekly_goal_check.prompt: Read ~/.koda/goals.md. For each goal, check progress against the target. Report the biggest gap in Discord. Propose 1-3 tasks that would close it.

healthcheck.prompt: Call the check_health tool. If any issue is found, report it in Discord. If everything is fine, log silently — do not post to Discord.

memory_consolidation.prompt: Review observations from today. Promote any durable patterns (ones that would still be true next week) to learnings.md. Keep learnings.md under 200 lines — delete or merge old entries if needed.

example_approval_task._comment: Example of an 'approval' task — Koda will draft something and wait for your Discord approval before acting. Delete or customize.
example_approval_task.prompt: Draft 3 tweets about recent work from observations.md. Send drafts to Discord for approval. Do NOT post until approved.
